users.bio: Media systems operator and guest on Episode 14.
Practical systems for small creator teams.
Podcast editing tips and production advice.
Metadata and RSS tooling for publishers.
Collaboration and review tooling for small podcast teams.

posts.text: Small podcast teams do not need more AI. They need fewer invisible handoffs. If your transcript, description, clips, and publish checklist all live in different places, release quality becomes luck. Curious how other teams handle this.
Good chapter markers do more for retention than most podcasters realize. Listeners want to jump straight to the part that solves today's problem.
The phrase 'final_v2_real_final.wav' is not a joke. It is a process smell. Naming conventions are editorial infrastructure.
@maya_chen Exactly. Most workflow debt is really state-management debt.
Metadata drift is one of the quietest ways to degrade a show. Your title, description, artwork, and chapters have to agree.
If you are still publishing from a raw transcript, you are making every downstream asset harder to trust. Clean transcripts are not busywork. They are infrastructure.
We just rolled out a cleaner review handoff flow for episode notes, sponsor copy, and publish approvals. Small teams need fewer bottlenecks, not more tabs.

direct_messages.text: Happy to boost the episode once it's live.
